title: centrolepis tenuior
intro: Centrolepis tenuior, also known as the 'thread fern', is a type of small, delicate, and rare flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been utilized in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as having potential antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Centrolepis tenuior include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions in a holistic and natural manner.
